title: Peltostigma pteleoides
what_description: Peltostigma pteleoides is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Asia. It is a shrub or small tree that grows up to 15 meters in height, with a straight and columnar stem and a broad, rounded crown. The leaves are alternate, simple, and elliptical in shape, measuring 5-18 cm in length and 2-6 cm in width, with a glabrous surface and a cuneate base. The flowers are yellow, 3-5-merous, and in small clusters, with a 5-lobed calyx and a 5-lobed corolla. The fruit is a drupe, measuring 1-2 cm in length, with a single seed and a smooth, glabrous surface.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Peltostigma pteleoides are inflammation, fever, diarrhea, rheumatism, and hypertension. Peltostigma pteleoides is used to treat gastroenteritis and dysentery due to its antidiarrheal properties, as well as cough and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. It is also used to treat digestive issues, such as dyspepsia and gastritis, and to reduce fever. Additionally, Peltostigma pteleoides has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that make it effective against skin infections, eczema, and skin irritation. Its wound-healing properties also make it a treatment option for skin infections and wounds.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Peltostigma pteleoides are attributed to its ability to combat various health issues, with its antimicrobial properties being effective in inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, which is due to the presence of flavonoids and saponins in the plant. The antioxidant properties of Peltostigma pteleoides play a crucial role in neutralizing free radicals and protecting the body from oxidative stress, while also contributing to its anti-inflammatory properties by reducing inflammation and alleviating pain. The analgesic properties of this plant are responsible for its ability to relieve pain and discomfort, which is attributed to the presence of volatile oils. Additionally, the cardioprotective properties of Peltostigma pteleoides help in preventing heart-related issues by protecting the heart from damage and promoting overall cardiovascular health. Furthermore, the bioactive compounds present in the plant, including flavonoids and saponins, contribute to its medicinal properties.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Peltostigma pteleoides are flavonoids which include quercetin and kaempferol that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Tannins present in the plant have been reported to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Phenolic acids such as ferulic acid have been isolated from Peltostigma pteleoides and have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Saponins from the plant have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities. Alkaloids such as pteleine have been isolated from the plant and have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Peltostigma pteleoides are derived from its leaves and bark, used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Infusion is made from the leaves and is used as a treatment for fever and respiratory issues due to its antipyretic and expectorant properties. Decoction is prepared from the bark and is used to alleviate stomach issues and diarrhea due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. Tincture is made from the leaves and bark and is used as a treatment for various conditions including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and bark are also used to prepare tea and salve for topical application to alleviate skin conditions and wounds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Peltostigma pteleoides improperly include developing diarrhea due to its high saponin content, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation. It may also interact with medications such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications, leading to adverse reactions. Peltostigma pteleoides can trigger allergic reactions in some individuals, causing symptoms such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Improper use of Peltostigma pteleoides can cause hypotension due to its vasodilatory effects, leading to a drop in blood pressure. Additionally, Peltostigma pteleoides may cause nausea and vomiting due to its emetic properties, which can be exacerbated by its saponin content.
uses: Peltostigma pteleoides, also known as the false sterculia, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's bark, roots, and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antiviral and antioxidant activities. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.
taxonomy: Peltostigma pteleoides belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. This plant is classified under the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. It is a species of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. Peltostigma pteleoides is a part of the genus Peltostigma, which comprises a limited number of species. The classification of Peltostigma pteleoides is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.
benefits: Peltostigma pteleoides is known to have various health benefits. It is traditionally used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other digestive issues. The plant is also said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain. Additionally, it is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs.
